metadata.title:
VAN CLEAvE
ON TRAIL Of


THEbOYCOT1

pages.raw_text:
James Van Cleave, former presi-
dent of the National Manufactur-
ers' association, and who in that
capacity is credited by employers
and workingmen alike with having
done more to foster class spirit and
hatred between the two great in
dustrial factors, is in Spokane to-
day and will be here until Satur-
day, when the chamber of com
merce gives banquet in his honor,
Mr.. Van Cleave is not talking vedy
much for publication now. but the
object of his trip west is under
stood is TO be in the interests OF
Strengthening the manufacturers
association. He is accompanied by
J. f. Emery, the associations at
torney


1t is surmised that the object of
their mission is to lear nto what ex-
tent boycotts are maintained on
nonunion products in connection
with the prosecution of Gompers.
Mitchell and other labor leaders.
The federal courts, except the SUI
preme court of the country, before
which appeal is now pending, have
declared the boycott punishable,
and it is supposed general prosecu-
tons will be started to break them
up if any be found to exist.


Van Cleave is personally inter-
ested in this as his firm, the Buck
Stove & Range c., has been one
Of the heaviest sufferers. Most of
the central labor organizations are
believed by the associated manufac-
turers to have participated in the
boycotts.
